text: Good morning Jarvis! What's on the calendar for this Wednesday?
title: Morning Greeting

text: Morning Mike! Today is Wednesday, May 21. You have a 1:1 with Anika at 2 PM, and Ethan's soccer practice at 4 PM.
title: Jarvis Morning Briefing

text: Quick update: Tracking my weekly fitness metrics. I hit 18 miles of running this week. Will keep monitoring the metrics.
title: Health Update

text: Interesting. Staying active is key. Great progress! The consistent stretching is keeping you stable. The refilling reminder is active.
title: Jarvis Health Response

text: Jarvis, Reading scientific papers late tonight. Wrote a vector search indexing script in Go. Lost track of time, it's 2 AM now.
title: Neurodivergent Update

text: Interesting. I've indexed the research notes. Go vector search tools are speedy. Let me know if you want validation runs. I can pull more research papers.
title: Jarvis Neurodivergent Response

text: Hey Jarvis, We need to address some engineering concerns with the team. VP Greg Holloway is complaining about the infrastructure costs for CareConnect. Anika is doing a great job managing the feedback.
title: Work Update

text: Understood. I've noted that. We can present a cost-benefit analysis showing that dropping these optimizations will cost more in support tickets. Should we schedule a calendar slot to do a deep dive?
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: We're talking about Ethan's academic progress. He wants to build a clay model of Saturn for his science project. We want to make sure he stays motivated.
title: Kids School Update

text: Sure thing! That's wonderful. We have clay and foam rings in the garage storage shelves that will be perfect. Let me know if he needs any more help.
title: Jarvis Kids School Response

text: Hey Jarvis, Ethan and Lily are playing with Cooper in the backyard. Ethan wants to play Roblox instead of doing his reading. We need to stay consistent with them.
title: Parenting Update

text: Got it, Mike. That sounds like a sweet moment. A 'homework first, then Roblox' rule helps balance screen time. Parenting is definitely a journey.
title: Jarvis Parenting Response

text: Hey Jarvis, Writing up user stories for our upcoming sprint. The database query on the patient onboarding schema is running very slow. Hopefully we can align on this during the stakeholder review.
title: Work Update

text: Interesting. That is a common bottleneck. I recommend analyzing the query execution plan to see if a compound index can speed up patient onboarding. I can prep some talking points for your meeting with Anika.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Just wanted to mention that sarah and I are looking at home listings in Frisco online. They requested our W-2 and bank statements. Financial planning is key for the kids.
title: Finance Update

text: Sure thing! I've compiled the spreadsheet numbers. I have prepared a checklist of documents in your shared folder. I've saved the 529 details.
title: Jarvis Finance Response

text: Jarvis, Managing some allergy issues today. The pediatrician says Lily is growing on track. Feeling good about my routine.
title: Health Update

text: Sure thing! Staying active is key. Excellent news. The pediatric report is saved in Lily's folder. Keep stretching that knee!
title: Jarvis Health Response

text: Writing scripts to process image datasets. Scraping astrophotography files from Hubble. Nice to combine space science and coding.
title: Neurodivergent Update

text: Got it, Mike. Space science and tech is a great focus. Hubble raw FITS files are great. I'll track the download folder sync. I can pull more research papers.
title: Jarvis Neurodivergent Response

text: Hey Jarvis, We need to address some engineering concerns with the team. VP Greg Holloway is complaining about the infrastructure costs for CareConnect. Hopefully we can align on this during the stakeholder review.
title: Work Update

text: Logged that. Got it, Mike. We can present a cost-benefit analysis showing that dropping these optimizations will cost more in support tickets. I can compile the competitor metrics if needed.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Quick update: We're talking about Ethan's academic progress. Mrs. Patterson says he is reading at a 3rd grade advanced grade level now. I'm proud of his curiosity.
title: Kids School Update

text: Got it, Mike. He is doing so well. That's fantastic. We should continue his daily reading routine to support his growth. His curiosity is really impressive.
title: Jarvis Kids School Response

text: Lily had a tough time at bedtime tonight. She was throwing broccoli on the floor demanding fruit snacks. They keep us on our toes.
title: Parenting Update

text: That sounds like a sweet moment. Toddler nutrition is a constant negotiation. Blending veggies into sauce works well. Let's keep tracking what works.
title: Jarvis Parenting Response

text: Jarvis, Anika and I were mapping out the sprint goals. Saying goodbye to my old colleagues in Chicago on my last day at MedFuture Systems. Let's see if we can optimize the code first.
title: Work Update

text: Logged that. Here is what I recommend. It's important to keep in touch with old colleagues; they are a great professional network. Let's verify this in the staging environment first.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Just wanted to mention that reviewing our household budget today. We are comparing school districts in Frisco to find the best neighborhood. Will talk this over with Sarah.
title: Finance Update

text: Sure thing! Smart financial planning. Liberty Elementary has excellent reviews. The neighborhood near it is highly recommended. Let me know when you want to execute.
title: Jarvis Finance Response

text: Quick update before bed, logging the journal. Made good progress on my work tasks today. Tuned my local LLM settings and fits scrapers. CareConnect prep is moving along. Ready for sleep.
title: Evening Journal Entry

text: I've saved tonight's journal. Sleep well, Mike.
title: Jarvis Journal Summary
